title: More Nonsense Limerick 10
content:
There was an old person of Bromley,
    Whose ways were not cheerful or comely;
    He sate in the dust, 
    Eating spiders and crust,
    That unpleasing old person of Bromley.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Edward Lear delivers a powerful performance in "More Nonsense Limerick 10"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
